title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · Terminal Trace: Field Transform Tools
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux field transform tools increment in a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose cut, paste, tr, sed, awk, or a structured parser based on delimiter, record, escaping, and mutation requirements.
Apply sort keys, numeric/version ordering, stable behavior, locale, and adjacent duplicate rules before using uniq.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Field Transform Tools
steps.instruction:
theory case — Logs contain duplicate IDs, spaces, UTF-8 names, and severities high, low, and HIGH. Produce a stable 5-row summary with explicit locale, delimiter, regex, sort key, and duplicate policy.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Field Transform Tools contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Logs contain duplicate IDs, spaces, UTF-8 names, and severities high, low, and HIGH. Produce a stable 5-row summary with explicit locale, delimiter, regex, sort key, and duplicate policy. A defensible solution applies Field Transform Tools, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures.

content.code:
LC_ALL=C awk -F, 'NR > 1 { print $2, $1 }' data/incidents.csv \
  | sort -k1,1 -k2,2 \
  | uniq -c
